body_txt: The music business can be quite fickle. For every One-Hit Wonder , there's a fraction of acts who managed a streak of hits that lasts for decades, or at least a handful of big hits. Then there's the No-Hit Wonder, an artist who proves commercially successful or manages to have a long career without having a hit (defined here as managing to land in the Top 40 of the weekly music charts) at all. While there are other ways an artist can be considered successful (or a "wonder") besides having hits (such as album sales, concert attendance, major awards, or the like), it's considered rare for a music act to be considered successful without a hit, since album sales are generally driven by the popularity of individual songs and artists with no hits tend not to develop enough of a fanbase to make their concerts well-attended. In many cases, a No-Hit Wonder will have had singles that never went anywhere on the charts, but which are still well-known and well-loved by a large number of people. In the streaming age, many of the examples here are of artists who had at least one really long-term Sleeper Hit , with songs that have managed to sell millions of units or accrue billions of streams over the years but were never massively successful in a single week. See also Hitless Hit Album .
